layer_1_flower.intent: Total physical surrender in diesel-spiced bliss
layer_1_flower.lineage: Green Lantern X Double OG Chem

layer_2_essential_oil.blend_rationale: Black Pepper carries the dominant beta-caryophyllene. Sage matches the diesel aroma note. Lemon provides Limonene support. Lemongrass rounds the blend with a soft finish.

layer_3_music.spotify_prompt: A low-energy session. Total physical surrender in diesel-spiced bliss. Genres: ambient, dub, lo-fi hip hop, deep house. Tempo around 65-90 BPM.

layer_4_tea.name: Double Tea
layer_4_tea.description: Diesel and pine infused chamomile blend
method: Heat 8 oz filtered water to 185°F.
Add chamomile tea bag and steep for 5 minutes.
Add charcoal-roasted hojicha and let infuse for 1 minute.
Add fresh rosemary sprig and let infuse for 1 minute.
Strain into a warm teacup.
Stir in honey and serve.

mocktail.name: Double Fizz
method: Fill a rocks glass with large ice cubes.
Combine 2 oz charred rosemary sprig and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Add 0.5 oz rosemary syrup.
Shake for 8 seconds.
Strain into glass over fresh ice.
Top with 2 oz sparkling water and stir gently.
Garnish with diesel.

cocktail.name: Double Sour
method: Combine 2 oz bourbon, 1.5 oz charred rosemary sprig, and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker.
Add 0.5 oz rosemary syrup.
Dry shake for 5 seconds (if using egg white).
Add ice and shake for 10 seconds.
Strain into a chilled coupe.
Garnish with diesel.

juice.name: Double Juice
method: Press 2 cups fresh apple through a cold press.
Press 1 green apple through the press.
Add 0.5 inch fresh ginger root to the press.
Combine all juices in a glass.
Add 0.5 oz raw honey and stir until dissolved.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

wellness_shot.name: Double Shot
method: Chill a shot glass in the freezer for 2 minutes.
In a mixing glass, combine 1 oz bourbon with 0.75 oz fresh apple juice.
Add 0.25 oz fresh lemon juice.
Stir with ice for 5 seconds.
Strain into the chilled shot glass and serve immediately.

snack.name: Medjool Dates Stuffed with Goat Cheese
snack.desc: Plump dates filled with herbed chèvre and topped with a walnut half and honey drizzle.

appetizer.name: Wild Mushroom Toast with Thyme
appetizer.desc: Sautéed mixed mushrooms on sourdough with fresh thyme and shaved Parmesan.

main.name: Lamb Tagine with Preserved Lemon
main.desc: Slow-braised lamb with apricots, olives, preserved lemon, and warming spices.

dessert.name: Cardamom Rice Pudding
dessert.desc: Creamy arborio rice pudding with cardamom, rosewater, and crushed pistachios.

cheese.name: Manchego & Membrillo
cheese.desc: Aged Manchego with quince paste, Marcona almonds, and crusty bread.

bath_bomb.base: baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch, Epsom salt
bath_bomb.extras: dried flower petals, oat milk powder

shower_steamer.activation: Place on shower floor away from direct stream. Steam activates essential oils for 5-10 minutes of aromatherapy.

layer_10_incense.burn_method: Place on heat-resistant surface in a ventilated room. Allow 30 seconds for the flame to establish before extinguishing to a smolder.
layer_10_incense.ritual_suggestion: Light before the session begins. Let the smoke settle the room while you prepare your total space.

layer_11_environment.spatial_setup: Low seating, soft throws, candles at eye level. Minimize overhead lighting.
layer_11_environment.scent_anchor: Essential oil blend via diffuser, started 30 minutes before session

layer_12_breathwork.timing: Begin with the diffuser. Complete all cycles before consumption.
layer_12_breathwork.guidance_text: Close your eyes. Breathe in through the nose for four counts, letting the scent fill your lungs. Hold gently for four. Release slowly through the mouth for eight counts, feeling gravity pull you deeper into your seat with each exhale.

layer_13_narrative.text:
25 drops of black pepper carry the dominant β-Caryophyllene through the room slowly — the way Greenshock Farms intended when they cultivated Double Lantern in Willits, Mendocino County. The lineage reads Green Lantern X Double OG Chem, but what arrives in the glass is something that transcends parentage: diesel, pine, and black pepper.

The second oil — Sage — adds 9 drops of brightness that matches the pine note in the flower itself. Physically Relaxing is not a command here but an invitation, the way the best strains suggest rather than insist. The body settles into heavy with the kind of molecular precision that only terpenes at 2.67% total can deliver.

In the dimly lit room, the essential oil blend has been working for thirty minutes before the flower arrives. The pre-load creates a terpene environment that the cannabis completes rather than initiates — your olfactory system already tuned to the frequency of β-Caryophyllene, already primed for total physical surrender in diesel-spiced bliss. The third oil adds depth. The fourth oil rounds everything into a finish that feels inevitable rather than constructed.

This is what Greenshock Farms grows in Willits, Mendocino County: not just cannabis, but context. The indica genetics express through terpenes that speak the same chemical language as rosemary in a Mendocino garden. The session settles into its final form — blissful, unhurried, complete — and the 4 oils that started it are now indistinguishable from the flower that finished it.

layer_14_location.terpene_walk: Begin at SF Botanical Garden — Fragrance Garden, diffuse essential oil blend 30 minutes before arrival. Walk slowly, breathing deeply through the nose to layer forest phytoncides over the pre-loaded terpene profile.

wine.terpene_bridge: Rotundone in Syrah shares the spicy, peppery character of β-caryophyllene — same aromatic family, different kingdoms.
wine.tasting_note: Dark fruit, cracked pepper, coastal minerality

wine.terpene_bridge: Zinfandel's brambly spice and warm pepper notes echo caryophyllene's warming, anti-inflammatory character.

beer.terpene_bridge: Belgian yeast esters produce spicy, peppery phenolics that echo caryophyllene's warming quality.
Centennial hops carry significant caryophyllene — cannabis and hops are Cannabaceae cousins.

layer_15_wine_beer.cannabaceae_note: Cannabis and hops (Humulus lupulus) are botanical cousins in the family Cannabaceae, sharing terpene synthesis pathways that produce identical aromatic molecules.
